title: Filhol (2020): A Human-Editable Sign Language Representation . . . and a Writing System?
subtitle: A review of semantic-layer insight, SignWriting misclassification, and overstated novelty claims
summary: Review essay on Filhol's 2020 representation proposal.
synopsis: This review argues that Filhol is right about the limits of video and the importance of editable, queryable signed-language representation, but wrong to treat SignWriting as merely phonographic and structurally inadequate. The paper's durable value is as semantic-layer prompting, not as a successful reclassification of SignWriting.
